description:
**Energy Potential for Hydropower**

Energy potential for hydropower for the current status (2020), with and without consideration of optimisation/refurbishment, and the **technical potential in future years (2040, 2070\)** at regional level, depicted at the level of catchment areas of Austria’s river basin.

The map contains information on following parameters: 

* **Energy (E)**: Annual electricity generated by hydropower in GWhel per year

* **Capacity (C)** Installed capacity of hydropower, in MWel

* **Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE):** Average indicative levelized cost per unit of generated electricity from hydropower, in EUR per MWhel

* **Market Value (MV):** Average indicative revenues that hydropower producers may gain from selling the electricity on the wholesale electricity market, in EUR per MWhel

**Technical potential** represents the maximum energy flow under physical and technical constraints, such as efficiency and spatial limitations. 

The map includes **potentials without the effects of climate change (WOCC)**, with **moderate climate change (RCP4.5 \- MOCC)** and **strong climate change effects (RCP8.5 – STCC)**.

The underlying study, “Renewable Energy Potentials in Austria for 2030 and 2040,” was conducted on behalf of the Austrian Climate and Energy Fund and was financed with appropriations from the former Federal Ministry for Climate Action, Environment, Energy, Mobility, Innovation and Technology (BMK).

[download raw data as GeoJSON](https://workspace-ui-public.gtif-austria.hub-otc.eox.at/api/public/share/public-4wazei3y-02/KLIEN-studie/hydro/hydro_EEPOT_W23.geojson)

[download raw data as CSV](https://workspace-ui-public.gtif-austria.hub-otc.eox.at/api/public/share/public-4wazei3y-02/KLIEN-studie/hydro/Hydro_EEPOT.csv)

**Please cite the source when using these data:**
G. Resch, B. Mayr, B., Gahleitner, E. Haslinger, S. Böhmer, M. B. Broer, C. Brendle, G. Eisenkölb, S. Nemetz, C. Elser-Amann, A. Storch, D. Trauner, P. Weiss, R. Wasserbauer, A. Stökl, A. Krenn, F. Mauthner, W. Gruber-Glatzl, J. Pfleger, A. Stöger, L. Kranzl, F. Radosits, F. Schipfer, P. Valent, 2026. Erneuerbare Energiepotenziale in Österreich für 2030 und 2040.
